Author: @al3xK1988
Comment_Content: I rarely write comments, but I can't hold back with this interview. The first half hour of the interview really surprised me. I didn't know this Trump, who also admits his inaccuracy and talks about his lack of experience, otherwise he knows everything better than others. That made me think for a brief moment and I had slight doubts. But my doubts quickly disappeared after I watched the whole interview. Trump only sees everything in black or white. For him, everyone is either good or bad, smart or stupid and he is, of course, the greatest. He doesn't believe in polls, only the ones that speak in his favor. He complains that some Democrats don't recognize the 2016 election, but orders a storming of the Capitol after his 2021 election defeat. And he also has this view when he talks about global issues such as the environment, the economy, wars, country relations. Joe Rogan said at the beginning: “You [D. Trump] said a lot of wild shit”. And he's absolutely right. Because that's exactly the kind of bullshit Trump expressed in this interview. And Joe Rogan really disappointed me in this interview that he didn't respond to this bullshit. He even agreed with this bullshit. Here are some examples: Trump on environmental impact. [00:53:00] “I had to do environmental impact studies.” Are you aware of his studies? I can't find any publications by Donald Trump on environmental issues. He says that China is polluting our environment. Yes, that's true, but without mentioning that the US is second in terms of CO2 emissions. Fish are causing water shortages in California [00:59:00} Furthermore, according to Trump, a species of fish is to blame for California's water supply problems! What nonsense. According to Trump, no water is coming from the north, from Canada to California, because that water is being diverted into the Pacific Ocean to protect a species of fish! Trump better look at the map and realize that Canada and California don't even share a border. A short time later, Joe Rogan asks if Trump has heard of Tulare Lake in California. And Trump immediately says: “They have so much water”. What now? No, Trump doesn't know this lake, but immediately claims that this lake was drained into the Pacific Ocean. OMG! Then they realize that the lake has dried up due to agricultural use. And when shown Google images, Trump responds with: “Oh woh, what a great system.” Apparently Google is a foreign word to him. But Google can quickly uncover a lot of “wild shit” Trump says. He demonstrates his lack of knowledge of search engines once again when he proudly shows the sheet with the chart on healthcare expenditure vs. life expectancy and a short time later sees the same chart on the monitor. He apparently thought he was the only one with this chart. Further examples: Wildfires: [01:04:00] Dead trees that are not cleared are responsible for wildfires in California. Because according to Trump, a living tree that has soaked up water cannot burn. Only dead trees and leaves on the ground cause forest fires. 🤦‍♂ Nuclear Power: [01:06:00] They talk about nuclear power as the cleanest source of energy. And they cite the accidents in Chernobyl and Fukushima as the only concern as to why nuclear power plants are not favored. But maybe it's because of the nuclear waste produced and the fact that there is no real repository for nuclear waste anywhere in the world. Think about it! Yes, but wind turbines are ugly, expensive and kill our birds. So dear migrants, not only you are killing our pets according to Trump, but the evil wind turbines are also causing bird graveyards. 🥴 Wars: [01:25:35] Trump would have prevented Russia's criminal invasion of Ukraine with two sentences. Trump would say, “Vladimir, you're not going in.” And the oil price of $40 would have prevented the invasion. What nonsense. Look at the global oil price statistics. Corona had a greater impact on falling oil prices than the sanctions against Russia after the invasion. The price of oil has even risen because many countries were unsettled by this war. After all, Russia is one of the largest oil producers in the world. Trump as airline pilot and defeater of ISIS [1:30:00] Apparently Trump sees no difference between ISIS and Taliban. I'll let you in on a secret: Taliban hate and fight ISIS supporters. He constantly confuses who he's fighting, where he's flying and who he's negotiating with. He prevents the killing of American soldiers with his appointment with Abdul [Ghani Baradar]. And when Trump leaves the post, soldiers are killed again. WTF? Let's look at the statistics of US soldiers killed in Afghanistan. 2016 > 13, 2017>15, 2018>14, 2019>24, 2020>11. So for Trump that's 77 US soldiers, not a single death. Incidentally, 13 soldiers died in 2021 when Biden took over. And of course Trump knows that number. Trump on tariffs and relocating factories to the USA. Apparently globalization is a foreign word for Trump. No country can grow successfully without global world trade and relations. Countries that strive for economic growth are dependent on each other. And threats of tariffs or payments of protection money are completely out of line. Why are iPhones not produced in the USA? Quite simply because production in China is still cheaper. If the entire production was moved to the USA, the iPhone would be many times more expensive. TSMC in Taiwan produce great microchips that the whole world needs. And did you know that the complex machines that play an important role in production come from the Netherlands (ASML)? Without these machines, high-quality chips cannot be produced. That's how the world works. We are dependent on each other. I could go on and on like this. Because as I said, Joe Rogan is absolutely right about one thing. Trump talks a lot of “wild shit”. Just a real shame that for some reason Joe Rogan doesn't step in and take him to task or fact check his statements. Because he does that in his other interviews where he has a different opinion. And by the way, as Joe Rogan correctly points out. Trump has the right to his own opinion BUT not his own facts. Too bad, Joe. 😔

Comment_Content: 37:53 yeah I agree a lot of cops are good but the majority that I have met they are looking for a reason to arrest me and I didn't do anything wrong I'm being respectful but they are trying to put me in jail. This is going to be a novel but good example. This might have been dumb of me but I go into Walmart I don't want to touch the carts that they have because I think they're gross so I just grab a Walmart bag. I go and pick up a few items stick it in the bag I go and get a hard drive that needs a key to get into because it's locked so I get an employee to unlock it for me Outback there is a place where you can purchase everything so I take everything out of the bag they scan it I pay I leave. I go to the car to get in to cop cars block me in so I can't leave. I get out of the car and I go what seems to be the problem officers. This little short cop goes I don't know you tell me. I'm like I don't know that's why I'm asking he goes what's in the other bag I go what other bag. He goes you know what bag what's in the other bag. I go the bag I'm holding. This guy's a total a-hole acting like he's on the show 24 so I explained to him. I went into the store I grabbed this said bag I put items into this bag I went to purchase everything that's in this bag and I left the store that's the only bag and that's what I did he goes I don't believe you you better be telling me the truth I'm going in there right now and I'm looking at the cameras. The other cop that standing outside with him very nice ask me for my ID and I said no there's no reason for that then he asked me well can I look in the bag I said absolutely. He looked at the items he looked at the receipt was like there's not a problem I said exactly. Meanwhile the other cops in the store he comes out and with this attitude he's like all right you can go. I forgot to say before he came out I was talking to the other officer and I was like your partner is a dick huh. This other officer the nice one was like well he is a nice guy like he's trying to convince himself that this guy's a nice guy but he's not. He's just the bully and he's just trying to get his quota. There's other examples I can give but I'd be talking way too much but he was a chief of police and he got fired so that should tell you something right there but he was always a dick. Will I try to do is be respectful and now I always record because who knows if I didn't record that how would that situation went with Napoleon Dynamite. For all I know he would have tackled me and put me in handcuffs for absolutely zero reason the taller guy extremely nice polite I was polite the entire time I was respectful the entire time to both of them. But some cops man they really do suck. But then you do have the few that are really good cops there is one in my hometown that knew me and my friend and he helped me out several times where he could have arrested me 1,000 % I was in the wrong but instead he let me go home. When one of my friends died I was having a panic attack I called an ambulance he showed up. We were talking about my friend and he was like man sorry to hear about that and we had a decent conversation. Honestly I hope he's still on the 4th and I would like to have him be chief I mean the stuff that he let me go for it wasn't really a big deal but if he wanted to he could 100% arrest me if you choose to but luckily he didn't so like I said I have met more crappy cops than good cops. How do you fix that. Maybe get rid of the quota cops shouldn't be out looking to arrest you they should be looking to help you and I think 40% of the time they are trying to help you the other they want to arrest you I wish the numbers were flipped. Again sorry for the horrible punctuation and the long-ass novel but that's just my opinion and situation that I've been in

Author: @elozjr09
Comment_Content: Prolly gonna get downvoted to hell, but I have to chime in. Outside of his “weaving” from topic to topic, I was following along and even laughed a couple times. Trump’s a personable guy, there’s no question about that. But once he started talking about wind turbines, he dropped the ball hard. 1:10:50 I’m a former Onshore Wind Performance Engineer. Wind turbines actually produce a lot of energy, enough to power 1,000s of homes continuously (depending on their MW rating, and the amount of turbines present at a particular site). And typical FSA contracts between OEMs and asset owners are 10-15 years. Yeah, they’re not as visually appealing, but land owners are compensated for occupying their land. I also helped build curtailment parameters for wind turbines that protected birds and bats. Trump’s claim that wind farms are “bird cemeteries” is very overblown here. And wind turbine operators pay huge fines when birds are killed (which are called “takes” in the industry). Those fees can sometimes be $100k+ for a single bird, depending on the species killed. Also, wind turbines don’t just “rust”, they either get repowered or decommissioned. It’s true that they’re not as green as other renewable energy sources because they required oil to function, but all-in-all, that point in the conversation was just straight up BS. All in all, it’s hard to understand what’s really motivating him. I want to believe he’s going to do great things once he takes office, but the amount of rambling and truth-stretching he’s doing here is worrying. I hope he kills it, though.

Comment_Content: 2:41:20 Pre 2017 US companies making above 5mil in revenue were subject to 35% tax on revenue, TC&JA changed that to 21%. Big corporations didn't need a 14% tax cut, the lower class needed that. I used to be as fiscally conservative as they come, but I think the pendulum has swung way too far on the side of the wealthy and big corporations. I understand that you'll perform better economically if you capitalize your most useful/productive cohort, (which is usually the wealthy and big corps) but the lower end consumer is falling apart and we are a consumer driven economy. We can only stretch inequality so far before you get social unrest. IMO both sides are too extreme on their policy proposals, neither candidate is getting my vote this time around. Edit: I'm sick of this "oh the wealthy will just leave" argument against raising taxes for the wealthy. Great, install a 99% exit tax before implementing any other changes, that'll take care of that. I'm telling you the level of complacency of our wealthy class is rivaling that of the Russia in 1917. Selling out the posterity so they can overstimulate now, leaving behind 50% of your population that doesn't own assets. Fucking incredible, you truly can't count on anything in this world except for self interest.

Author: @jaysleeper264
Comment_Content: Regarding the Lake around 1:01:35. Tulare Lake (Too-lair-ee). The water does not come from Canada, it comes from the Sierras. It no longer fills due to damming all along the western side of the sierras. This is done up and down the valley to capture snow melt and much was done in the earlier 1900s. The purpose was store wet year's water to be used in dry years. This gave farmers the insurance needed to begin wide spread planting of permanent crops. The lake still fills from time to time if there are copious amounts of winter snow in the Sierras. 1983, which is mention, was such a year. 2023 was as well. 1997 or 8 was a year where some water was there but not considered a filling. When it fills now it covers farm land and houses that have all been built on the dry bed. It floods when the copious winter snow begins to melt during the exceptionally wet years and eventually over takes the down stream dams and rivers and breaches levees. 2023 flooding has some cool pictures if anyone is interested. Have a good day.

Author: @richardwdolan
Comment_Content: Whale biologist here! Listening for the environmental takes on resource management. 1:11:20 Trump claims “they say the noise (of windmills) drives the whales crazy”. This is in regard to a stranding of 23 large whales who washed up in NY and NJ in 2023. Speaking in facts, 16 of these animals had wounds conclusive with vessel strike, and no mortalities were found to have injuries synonymous with noise. Sonar tests and seismic surveying conducted by the US Navy in 1992 caused hemorrhaging in the ears and brains of beaked whales, so if windmills were killing whales, biologists have a means of comparison for drawing this conclusion. The whales are not “going crazy”. Tissue samples can be collected to test for stress hormones, or cortisol, in dead whales. This is detected highest in humpbacks and right whales who have been injured or killed by fishing gear, but cortisol levels were not found notable elevated in the 2023 strandings. If you want to learn what ACTUALLY is killing whales, please refer to “We Are All Whalers” by Michael J Moore and “War of the Whales” by Joshua Horwitz. Hint: Windmills are not killing whales. Entrapment in fishing gear, collisions with boats, pollution, and fluctuations in the food chain kill whales. P.S. I’m not being paid by “Big Wind LLC”, we marine biologist struggle to even keep the lights on!!

Author: @MintyLime
Comment_Content: 45:56 Starlink really is incredible. I've lived rurally my entire life, 26 years old. Never had or used internet exceeding 700KB/s. AT&T sucked up so much of our money for essentially nothing, and then here comes along Starlink with a better deal and 50MB/s download. I still haven't gotten used to the idea of actually being able to use the internet at all times of the day and do so reliably without interruption or slow downs. A year ago I spotted some new satellites they launched and it's just cool to actually see them and know that someone's actually doing something useful with these technologies for every day people. He could make a fortune off people with few other options and yet it's way more affordable than the scams wire companies are running. And it's actually high speed unlike satellite internet companies of the past.
